question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=CONSENSUS_SUBMIT_MESSAGE` and inject a crafted `message` string containing binary data or null bytes in the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit malformed HCS messages that corrupt topic state on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=TOKEN_MINT` and set an extremely large `amount` in the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit token mint transactions that inflate token supply beyond intended limits?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=SCHEDULE_SIGN` and inject a target `scheduleId` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit schedule sign transactions that prematurely execute pending scheduled transactions on the network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=TOKEN_FREEZE` and inject a target `accountId` and `tokenId` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit token freeze transactions that lock token balances of arbitrary accounts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=TOKEN_REVOKE_KYC` and inject a target `accountId` and `tokenId` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit KYC revocation transactions that strip KYC status from arbitrary accounts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=TOKEN_PAUSE` and inject a target `tokenId` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit token pause transactions that halt all transfers of a token across the entire network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=ACCOUNT_UPDATE` and inject a crafted `key` or `autoRenewPeriod` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit account update transactions that modify the operator account's key or settings?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=CONSENSUS_UPDATE_TOPIC` and inject a crafted `adminKey` or `submitKey` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit topic update transactions that change access control on HCS topics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=TOKEN_UPDATE` and inject a crafted `adminKey` or `supplyKey` via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit token update transactions that transfer control of a token to an attacker-controlled key?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `type=SCHEDULE_CREATE` and inject a crafted inner transaction via the `properties` map, causing the monitor to submit schedule creation transactions that wrap attacker-defined transactions for later execution on the network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user configure multiple scenarios with the same `type` but different `properties` entries, causing the `CompositeTransactionGenerator` to submit conflicting transactions (e.g., simultaneous TOKEN_FREEZE and TOKEN_UNFREEZE for the same token) that create inconsistent on-chain state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user set `receiptPercent=1.0` in a `PublishScenarioProperties` entry, causing `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.next()` to always set `receipt=true` in every `PublishRequest`, forcing the monitor to query receipts for every submitted transaction and exhausting network query capacity?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user set `recordPercent=1.0` in a `PublishScenarioProperties` entry, causing `processTransactionResponse()` in `TransactionPublisher` to always execute a `TransactionRecordQuery` with a fixed `setQueryPayment(Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR))`, draining the operator's HBAR balance through excessive record query fees?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user set `timeout` to `Duration.ofSeconds(1)` (the minimum) in a `PublishScenarioProperties` entry, causing `TransactionPublisher.publish()` to time out nearly all transactions via `.timeout(properties.getTimeout())`, generating a flood of `PublishException` errors that mask real transaction tampering?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user set `tps` to an extremely large double value (no `@Max` constraint, only `@Min(0)`) in a `PublishScenarioProperties` entry, causing `CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild()` to create a `RateLimiter` with an unbounded rate and flood the Hedera network with transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user set `maxMemoLength=13` (the minimum) in a `PublishScenarioProperties` entry, causing `PublishScenario.getMemo()` to truncate the memo to only the timestamp portion via `StringUtils.truncate()`, stripping the scenario name from all submitted transaction memos and making transactions unattributable?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user set `logResponse=true` in a `PublishScenarioProperties` entry, causing `TransactionPublisher.publish()` to log every transaction response at INFO level, leaking transaction IDs, receipt statuses, and account information to log aggregation systems accessible to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `properties` map entry with an expression-injection payload (e.g., `${...}`) into a `PublishScenarioProperties.properties` map, which is processed by `ExpressionConverter.convert()` in `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.convert()`, causing server-side expression evaluation that tampers with the generated transaction parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `properties` map entry with a key that matches a sensitive field name (e.g., `privateKey`, `operatorKey`) into `PublishScenarioProperties.properties`, which is deserialized by `OBJECT_MAPPER.convertValue()` in `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.convert()` with `ACCEPT_CASE_INSENSITIVE_PROPERTIES=true`, overwriting security-sensitive supplier fields?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user inject unknown properties into `PublishScenarioProperties.properties` that are silently ignored by `OBJECT_MAPPER` (configured with `FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=false`), masking configuration errors and causing the transaction supplier to use default values that submit unintended transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user set `retry.maxAttempts` to `Long.MAX_VALUE` in a `PublishScenarioProperties` entry, causing `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator` to set `setMaxAttempts(Integer.MAX_VALUE)` on every transaction (via `(int) properties.getRetry().getMaxAttempts()`), causing the SDK to retry failed transactions indefinitely and submit duplicate transactions to the network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getScenarios()] Can an unprivileged external user set `limit=0` in a `PublishScenarioProperties` entry, causing `getLimit()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE` (via `limit > 0 ? limit : Long.MAX_VALUE`), making the scenario run indefinitely and submit an unbounded number of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `scenarios` map with a null value for a scenario entry, bypassing the `@Valid` constraint on the map values (which only validates non-null entries), causing a `NullPointerException` in `scenarios.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` that prevents the application from starting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `scenarios` map with a very large number of entries (no `@Size` constraint on the map), causing `validate()` to iterate over all entries and `CompositeTransactionGenerator` to instantiate a generator per scenario, exhausting memory and preventing transaction submission?
